title: cistus villosus var. corsicus
intro: Cistus villosus var. corsicus, also known as Corsican Rockrose, is a perennial subshrub that belongs to the Cistus genus, a group of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Cistus villosus var. corsicus include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce anxiety and stress, and boost the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and its essential oil is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation.
